diagrams.title: Kernel and Image in Transformation Mapping
diagrams.description: Shows domain V mapping to codomain W with kernel (pre-image of zero) in V and image (output set) in W
elements: Arrow from V to W labeled T
Kernel subset inside V labeled ker(T)
Image subset inside W labeled im(T)
Zero vector in both domains marked
diagrams.purpose: Visualize where kernel and image sit relative to transformation

diagrams.title: Rank-Nullity Theorem Pie Chart
diagrams.description: Domain V is split into kernel and complement, where dim(V) = dim(ker) + dim(im)
elements: Outer annulus labeled complement (isomorphic to im)
diagrams.purpose: Understand the dimension decomposition theorem visually

diagrams.title: One-to-One vs Onto Transformations
diagrams.description: Comparison of injective (1-1) and surjective (onto) transformations with kernel and image visualization
elements: 1-1 case: kernel = {0}, multiple inputs give unique outputs
Onto case: image = codomain, every output has preimage
Both 1-1 and onto: bijective case with kernel={0}, im=W
diagrams.purpose: Differentiate between one-to-one and onto properties using kernel/image
